extraction.one_line_summary: PHILIPSEN Jasper won a chaotic sprint finish after THOMAS Benjamin's late solo attack nearly held off the disorganized peloton in the final 500 meters.
tactical_patterns: VAN AERT Wout rode early breakaway himself to contest intermediate sprint points, revealing jumbo-visma willingness to fracture team cohesion for green jersey ambition.
THOMAS Benjamin's sub-500m solo attack nearly succeeded because sprint teams were too exhausted/disorganized after chaotic stage to mount coherent chase.
VINGEGAARD Jonas caught in splits without teammates, exposing vulnerability when POGAČAR's team attacks aggressively even on non-GC stages.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Team allowed him breakaway duty for green jersey points despite domestique obligations, confirming personal ambition prioritized over yellow jersey support.
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: First tour stage win after top-5 finishes stages 2-4, showing patience and form building across three weeks.
rider_signals.THOMAS Benjamin: positive: Nearly stole stage with sub-500m attack against depleted sprinters, revealing late-race aggression teams won't expect from domestique.
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: Bike exchange put full leadout effort but Dylan faded badly in final meters after hard chase.
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: negative: Caught without teammates in mid-stage splits after UAE aggressive riding, preview of isolation risk in final week.
